qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mirror settings SSRF bypasses local-address validation enforced during migration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an SSRF vulnerability in mirror settings that bypasses local-address validation, which is unrelated to the advisory-described path traversal in wiki page update. Different vulnerability class (SSRF vs path traversal), different affected component (mirror settings vs wiki editing), different root cause (missing SSRF validation in settings endpoint vs unsafe path.Join with os.Remove using unsanitized old_title).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in the wiki page update functionality (internal/database/wiki.go) where the old_title parameter is used unsafely in os.Remove(path.Join(localPath, oldTitle+".md")), allowing arbitrary file deletion. The qualifying finding describes an SSRF bypass in mirror settings, which is a completely different vulnerability class, component, and root cause. The finding does not reference the wiki component, the old_title parameter, or the path traversal vulnerability mechanism described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory describes path traversal in wiki page update via old_title parameter in internal/database/wiki.go:114
Advisory root cause: os.Remove(path.Join(localPath, oldTitle+".md")) without sanitization of old_title
mirror-settings-ssrf finding: SSRF bypass in mirror settings (different component, different vulnerability class)
Finding does not mention wiki functionality, old_title parameter, or path traversal in file deletion context
